sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Avere - FXT and vFXT/Avere vFXT Quick Reference Sheet
symptom: Write-heavy workload with ReadOnly cache policy causes NFS slot exhaustion, cache recycling, and high client-facing latency on Avere vFXT
rootCause: Cache policy set to ReadOnly while workload is write-heavy (>20% writes); write attempts cause permission errors or backpressure
solution: Change cache policy to ReadWrite or WriteBack to match the workload pattern; ReadOnly is designed for ~80% read / 20% write workloads

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Avere - FXT and vFXT/Avere vFXT Quick Reference Sheet
symptom: Avere vFXT cache full with increased latency and 'recycling by space' visible on dashboard
rootCause: Cache storage capacity exceeded, triggering cache eviction (recycling by space) which competes with active I/O
solution: Monitor cache fullness via dashboard; add vFXT nodes to expand capacity, or adjust caching policy to reduce cache footprint

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Avere - FXT and vFXT/Avere vFXT Quick Reference Sheet
symptom: Long-running revocations and stuck NFS operations on Avere vFXT node; conditions remain active on dashboard
rootCause: NFS operations stuck due to stale locks or revocations not clearing on a specific node
solution: Restart services on the node that first raised the condition to release stuck ops; use 'averecmd support.executeNormalMode node gsisupportbundle' to collect evidence first

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Avere - FXT and vFXT/Avere vFXT Quick Reference Sheet
symptom: Swap pressure and performance degradation on Avere vFXT nodes with very long uptime
rootCause: Very long node uptime leads to accumulated swap usage and memory fragmentation drift
solution: Plan periodic node reboots as maintenance hygiene; use HA-mode reboot for minimal disruption or simultaneous reboot in maintenance window for faster completion

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Avere - FXT and vFXT/Troubleshooting/RPC Slot Exhaustion - NFS
symptom: High client-facing latency on Avere vFXT with RPC slot exhaustion; req.hunt and req.asyncappend stats incrementing significantly, cwndlimit non-zero
rootCause: NFS RPC queue slots (default 256 per TCP connection) fully consumed due to delays between cluster nodes and core filer, causing operations to back up in the send queue
solution: Increase TCP connection multiplier (massX.nfsConnMult custom setting) from default 4 to 8 (max 23, typically ≤16); alternatively increase cluster IPs to create additional TCP connections. Verify core filer utilization <80% and no network congestion first.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Avere - FXT and vFXT/Troubleshooting/RPC Slot Exhaustion - NFS
symptom: Avere vFXT high latency with recv.timerExpire counter incrementing (non-zero), often misdiagnosed as RPC slot exhaustion
rootCause: Core filer not responding to NFS operations within NfsBackEndTimeout (55s+); this is NOT RPC slot exhaustion but a core filer response failure
solution: Collect packet capture between cluster and core filer for analysis; investigate core filer health and network path; do NOT increase nfsConnMult as this is a different root cause from RPC slot exhaustion

symptom: Azure HPC Cache throughput cannot be changed after creation; customer needs more performance but cannot scale up or add nodes
rootCause: HPC Cache throughput/SKU is fixed at creation and cannot be scaled up or out by adding nodes, unlike Avere vFXT
solution: Destroy and recreate the HPC Cache with a larger throughput configuration/SKU; plan capacity requirements before initial deployment
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Avere - HPC Cache/HPC Quick Reference Sheet

symptom: Azure HPC Cache queued requests, stuck operations, and latency spikes with all request slots consumed
rootCause: Slot exhaustion — all NFS request slots fully consumed, causing operations to queue and back up
solution: Align caching policy to workload, smooth client concurrency, cautiously increase slots only if backend and network can handle it; verify backend utilization <80%
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Avere - HPC Cache/HPC Quick Reference Sheet

symptom: Azure HPC Cache elevated frontend latency caused by slow backend storage target
rootCause: Backend storage target (core filer) slowdowns propagate as elevated frontend (client-facing) latency; cache cannot mask backend delays beyond cached data
solution: Compare frontend vs storage target latency on Virtual Dashboard; investigate backend storage health, network path, and core filer utilization independently
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Avere - HPC Cache/HPC Quick Reference Sheet

symptom: Azure Container Storage (Arc) pod in CrashLoopBackOff — pod keeps restarting repeatedly
rootCause: Application failure or container misconfiguration causing the container process to exit with non-zero code on every restart
solution: 1) kubectl get pods -n <ns> to identify failing pod; 2) kubectl describe pod <name> -n <ns> for events; 3) kubectl logs <name> -n <ns> (--previous for last crash) to find root error
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Container Storage Enabled by Azure Arc/Common errors Kubernetes

symptom: Azure Container Storage (Arc) pods stuck in Pending state — cannot be scheduled
rootCause: Resource constraints (insufficient CPU/memory), unbound PersistentVolumeClaims, node taint not tolerated, or no schedulable nodes available
solution: 1) kubectl describe pod <name> -n <ns> for scheduling failure reason; 2) kubectl get nodes && kubectl describe node <name> to check node health and capacity; 3) Address specific issue: add resources, fix PVC bindings, or add/modify taints/tolerations
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Container Storage Enabled by Azure Arc/Common errors Kubernetes

symptom: Azure Container Storage (Arc) container fails to start with CreateContainerConfigError
rootCause: Missing ConfigMap, missing Secret (secretKeyRef), volume/volumeMount/subPath mismatch, or node missing expected host path
solution: 1) kubectl describe pod for Events section; 2) Check: configmap not found → create/fix ConfigMap; secret not found → create/fix Secret; subPath/volumeMount errors → fix volume spec; stat no such file → reschedule to correct node or fix path
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Container Storage Enabled by Azure Arc/Common errors Kubernetes

symptom: Azure Container Storage (Arc) Cloud Ingest subvolume cannot use private endpoints; customer expects private networking between Arc cluster and Azure Storage
rootCause: Cloud Ingest currently relies on public endpoints of Azure Storage; private endpoint/private networking is not supported
solution: Use public endpoints for Cloud Ingest traffic; advise customer this is a current product limitation with no private networking support available
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Container Storage Enabled by Azure Arc/FAQ Frequenly asked questions

symptom: Azure Data Box Disk not mounted or accessible on Linux after rsync process is killed or system is restarted; NTFS partition appears but cannot be mounted
rootCause: NTFS inconsistencies or NTFS journal file corruption caused by unclean unmount after process kill or system restart
solution: Run 'ntfsfix /dev/sdX' on the NTFS volume to fix common NTFS problems and reset the journal file, then remount the disk and resume the copy job
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Disk/Connect & Copy/Azure Databox Disk is not mounted or accessible after process is killed or system is restarted

symptom: Unable to mount Azure Data Box Disk on Oracle Linux 7.9; disk not recognized or mount fails on unsupported Linux distribution
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Disk/Connect & Copy/Unable to mount Azure Data Box Disk using Oracle Linux version 7.9
rootCause: Oracle Linux 7.9 is not in the supported OS list for Azure Data Box Disk. Only specific Linux distributions listed in the system requirements documentation are supported.
solution: Switch to a supported OS such as CentOS. Refer to the Data Box Disk system requirements page for the full list of supported Linux versions.

symptom: Azure Data Box Disk in security locked state; DC cannot upload data; CreateDCInfra fails with DiskSecurityLockedException
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Disk/Data Copy at Azure/Azure DC cannot upload the data from the Data Box Disk because the Azure Data Box Disk is in security locked state
rootCause: Abnormal number of system power drops in quick succession causes the disk to enter Security Locked state where all reads and writes fail. Power drops can be caused by sudden drive removal or insufficient USB hub power supply. Unrelated to BitLocker.
solution: The disk cannot be recovered from security locked state. A secure erase must be performed. Customer must create a new Data Box Disk order and redo the import process.

symptom: Data Box Disk copy to Azure stuck or frozen at same GB value for extended time; DriveCopyTimeout error in ASC; data offload not progressing
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Disk/Data Copy at Azure/Copy to Azure is stuck at Azure Data Box Disk offload
rootCause: Drive copy to Azure faulted due to DriveCopyTimeout. Underlying error is Storage Exception The Remote name could not be resolved, caused by DNS communication issues between device, server, and storage account in the datacenter.
solution: 1) Check ASC Resource Explorer for Upload Progress and EntityHealthState. 2) Check if an auto-generated WatchDog IcM already exists. 3) If not, create IcM with Data Box Data Path Engineering Team to have the disk reloaded on another server for retry.

symptom: Customer returned Data Box Disk with their own shipping label instead of Microsoft-provided label; no updates on order status; data transfer not starting
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Disk/Device Return - Logistics/The customer has returned the device with another label and has not received any updates on Data Box Disk order
rootCause: When a Data Box device is returned using a shipping label other than the one provided by Microsoft, the return shipment is not linked to the Data Box order, preventing order status updates and delaying data transfer.
solution: 1) Get tracking information from customer shipment. 2) Create IcM with Data Box Operations Team with order details and tracking info. 3) Operations team notifies datacenter to locate package and start data transfer. Advise customer to always contact MS support before using own shipping label.

symptom: Subfolders created inside AzureFile folder disappear on Data Box Disk during validation; Robocopy completed successfully but folders appear missing
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Disk/Disk Validation/Subfolders created inside the AzureFile folder disappear on Azure Data Box Disk during Validation
rootCause: The Data Box Disk Validation Tool may lack permissions to view the folders, or the folders are set as hidden by the system after copy.
solution: Navigate to the affected folder on the Data Box Disk and run dir E:\AzureFile from command prompt to verify subfolders are present. Folders may be hidden or have restricted permissions but are still intact.

symptom: Unlocking Data Box Disk on Red Hat Linux (RHEL) 7.x fails with passkey error; logs show dislocker-file was not created
rootCause: DataBoxDiskUnlock_Prep.sh script contains broken URL (HTTP 404) for epel-release RPM package, causing required package dependencies to not install
solution: Manually download epel-release RPM from valid URL: wget https://dl.fedoraproject.org/pub/epel/epel-release-latest-7.noarch.rpm, then install with yum install ./epel-release-latest-7.noarch.rpm, then re-run the Data Box disk preparation script
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Disk/Hardware & Unlock/Unlocking a Data Box Disk on Red Hat Linux (RHEL) 7.x host fails with an error saying that the passkey is wrong

symptom: Data Box Disk not detected by host machine after connecting via USB
rootCause: Disk may be physically corrupt, or USB port/cable incompatibility
solution: 1) Try different USB port on host; 2) Connect to a different host machine; 3) Use a different USB cable; If still undetected after all steps, disk is likely corrupt — create IcM to request replacement disks
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Disk/Hardware & Unlock/Using USBTreeView tool to troubleshoot undetected disks or disks that cannot be unlocked

symptom: Data Box Disk detected by host but cannot be unlocked; disk may show as RAW or Unallocated in Disk Management
rootCause: USB port not USB 3.0, cable/host compatibility issue, or disk filesystem corruption showing as RAW/Unallocated
solution: 1) Ensure USB 3.0 port; 2) Try different host/cable; 3) Check Disk Management for RAW/Unallocated status; 4) Check Device Manager USB ports; 5) Use USBTreeView tool to collect port config report for further diagnosis; 6) If disk shows RAW, create IcM for replacement
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Disk/Hardware & Unlock/Using USBTreeView tool to troubleshoot undetected disks or disks that cannot be unlocked

symptom: Cannot change storage account associated with Data Box after order is processed
rootCause: By design — storage account is immutable once order is processed
solution: Customer must create a new order with the correct storage account and return the existing Data Box
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Disk/Ordering & Logistics/Data Box Disk FAQs

symptom: Source country not found in dropdown when creating Data Box order
rootCause: Data Box service is not available in the customer's country
solution: Workaround: 1) Use a branch office in a supported country to place order, then personally transport device; 2) Use self-managed shipping — pick up from datacenter in supported country, copy data, drop back off
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Disk/Ordering & Logistics/Data Box Disk FAQs

symptom: Data Box Gateway not uploading data to Azure cloud; Device events show "Could not upload file(s) from share" warning; files go to local cache but never reach cloud storage
rootCause: Blob Lifecycle Management policy on the storage account changed blobs to Archive tier. Archived blobs cannot be overwritten or appended by Data Box Gateway, causing upload failures with HTTP 409 BlobArchived error in hcsedgeagentwriter logs.
solution: Rehydrate archived blobs to Hot or Cool tier: (1) manually change tier per blob via Azure Portal or CLI, (2) delete the archiving lifecycle policy and create one that rehydrates to Hot/Cool, or (3) use PowerShell script with Set-AzStorageBlobTier to bulk-rehydrate all archived blobs in the storage account. High-priority rehydration takes ~1 hour; standard takes up to 15 hours.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Gateway/Connectivity & Performance/Data is not being uploaded to the Azure cloud on Data Box Gateway

symptom: Error 0x8007003A when copying files from Data Box Gateway share to local machine; files inaccessible despite connectivity mode set to Fully Connected
rootCause: Associated blobs in Azure Storage have been archived. Data Box Gateway cannot read archived blobs, producing error 0x8007003A (network path error) and HTTP 409 BlobArchived in hcsedgeagentreader/writer logs.
solution: Rehydrate archived blobs from Archive to Hot/Cool tier. Customer can use Azure Portal, CLI, or PowerShell to change blob tier. May need to engage Blob Storage support team via collaboration case.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Gateway/Connectivity & Performance/Getting Error 0x8007003A while copying files from Gateway share to local machine

symptom: Files on Data Box Gateway share take longer than normal to open; slow file access on first open
rootCause: By design, Data Box Gateway uploads data to Azure Storage and once local cache threshold is reached, removes files locally keeping only metadata. Files with Size on Disk = 0 reside only in Azure and must be downloaded on first access, causing slow open times.
solution: Check file properties: compare "Size" vs "Size on Disk" in File Explorer or PowerShell. Files with Size on Disk = 0 are cloud-only and will be slow on first open. After first access, file is cached locally and subsequent opens will be faster. This is expected behavior by design.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Gateway/Connectivity & Performance/Identifying which files are on the Local Cache (VM Data Disk) of Data Box Gateway

symptom: Upload Error 2003 on Azure Data Box Gateway; upload failure with nonTransientErrors in upload logs; hcsedgeagentwriter logs show exception for file paths with trailing period or space
rootCause: Directory or file name ends with a space or period, violating Windows local file/directory naming conventions. The Windows shell and UI cannot access such paths, causing the Data Box Gateway appliance to fail uploading files in those directories.
solution: Move affected files to a directory with a compliant name (no trailing space or period). Period as first character is acceptable. After renaming, files will upload normally. See Microsoft docs on file naming conventions: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/fileio/naming-a-file
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Gateway/Connectivity & Performance/Upload Error 2003 on Azure Data Box Gateway

symptom: Customer wants files to inherit destination domain permissions instead of retaining source ACLs when copying to Data Box via SMB; files keep source ACLs from Domain-A instead of inheriting Domain-B permissions
rootCause: By design, Data Box over SMB retains source ACLs and does not inherit destination permissions
solution: Three options: (1) Copy as-is then manually inherit permissions at destination using share-root inheritance; (2) Disable ACL forwarding in Data Box Web UI (loses timestamps/attributes too); (3) Create new directory on Data Box Share, set destination permissions before copying, use Robocopy with /DAT flag to prevent forwarding source permissions
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Applying ACL and Permissions at destination and removing source ACLs before data transfer to Data Box

symptom: Data Box copy job stuck/not progressing; hcsdpservice logs show Directory open failed with err: 5 and Enumeration of endpoint failed with error: 5
rootCause: Windows blocks reads by local users over the network due to UAC (User Access Control) restrictions
solution: Either (1) manually grant user permissions on the source folder via Properties > Security > Edit > Add user, or (2) lower UAC settings incrementally via Change User Account Control settings until copy job progresses
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Data Box Copy Job is not Progressing

symptom: Slow data copy to Data Box when copying millions of small files (especially <512KB); copy takes much longer than expected
rootCause: Small files incur overhead for per-file metadata verification during transfer, making throughput significantly slower than large files
solution: Use Robocopy with 2 sessions and 16 threads per session for small files <512KB to maximize throughput
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Data Copy very slow when copying to Data Box

symptom: Slow data copy to Data Box; host machine itself has performance issues affecting all copy operations
rootCause: Source host machine has underlying performance problems not specific to Data Box
solution: Isolate by copying same files to local volume or different machine; if host is confirmed slow, collect PerfMon trace and engage Windows performance team
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Data Copy very slow when copying to Data Box

symptom: Slow data copy to Data Box caused by 3rd party filter drivers interrupting the copy process
rootCause: 3rd party filter drivers (antivirus, backup agents, etc.) interfere with file I/O during Data Box copy
solution: Run fltmc command to list filter drivers; remove/unload 3rd party filter drivers one by one to isolate the culprit; contact 3rd party vendor for resolution
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Data Copy very slow when copying to Data Box

symptom: Metadata (ACLs, timestamps, attributes) not preserved when transferring data from Data Box to Blob Storage
rootCause: Blob Storage does not support NTFS file metadata (ACLs, timestamps, attributes); only Azure Files preserves this metadata
solution: Transfer data to Azure Files instead of Blob Storage to preserve metadata
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Metadata Preservation and Permissions Issues on Data Box

symptom: LastAccessTime, File_Attribute_Offline, and File_Attribute_Not_Content_Indexed metadata not transferred via Data Box
rootCause: By design, Data Box does not transfer these specific metadata attributes
solution: This is by-design behavior; these metadata types are not supported for transfer via Data Box
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Metadata Preservation and Permissions Issues on Data Box

symptom: ACLs not transferred during Data Box data copy over NFS protocol
rootCause: By design, NFS protocol does not transfer Windows-style ACLs
solution: This is by-design behavior; use SMB protocol instead of NFS if ACL preservation is required
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Metadata Preservation and Permissions Issues on Data Box

symptom: ACLs not transferred when using Data Box Data Copy service for file transfer
rootCause: Data Copy service does not preserve ACLs during data transfer
solution: Use Robocopy or other copy tool instead of the built-in Data Copy service to preserve ACLs
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Metadata Preservation and Permissions Issues on Data Box

symptom: Cannot copy data with ACL preservation on Data Box; permission errors during copy process
rootCause: User is not a Backup Operator or Backup Operator Privileges is disabled in Data Box Web UI
solution: Ensure user is part of the Backup Operator group and enable Backup Operator Privileges via Data Box Web UI
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Metadata Preservation and Permissions Issues on Data Box

symptom: ACLs and metadata not transferred during Data Box copy even when expected; Enable ACLs for Azure Files setting is disabled
rootCause: The Enable ACLs for Azure Files option is disabled in Data Box Web UI, preventing metadata transfer
solution: Enable ACLs for Azure Files in the Data Box local Web UI to transfer metadata including ACLs, file attributes, and timestamps
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Metadata Preservation and Permissions Issues on Data Box

symptom: Preserved timestamps not reflected correctly in Azure Portal after Data Box transfer; timestamps appear wrong
rootCause: Azure Portal displays REST Last Modified Timestamps instead of the preserved original file timestamps
solution: Mount the Azure file share and view files in Windows File Explorer to see the correctly preserved timestamps
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Metadata Preservation and Permissions Issues on Data Box

symptom: Files copied to wrong share on Data Box (e.g. _AzFiles instead of _BlockBlob); moving via File Explorer triggers slow copy instead of instant move
rootCause: User selected wrong data copy destination share on Data Box device; File Explorer cross-share move initiates full copy operation
solution: Use PowerShell Move-Item (Windows) or mv command (Linux) to move files between shares on the Data Box device, which is faster than File Explorer copy
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Moving files on Data Box device without having to re-copy

symptom: System error 1909 when connecting to Data Box via net use; account locked out after 5 invalid login attempts
rootCause: Data Box locks user account after 5 consecutive invalid authentication attempts; often caused by scripts, 3rd party tools (Commvault), or multiple clients using wrong/cached credentials
solution: Wait 30-40 minutes for auto-unlock; ensure correct credentials; check Security.evtx EventID 4625/4776/4740 to identify offending client IP; stop scripts or cached credential sources causing repeated failed logins
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Multiple users trying to access Data Box and getting locked out error 1909

symptom: Cohesity or 3rd party tool gets AccessDenied when running chown on Data Box NFS share; can only write as root
rootCause: Data Box NFS shares do not support chown (ownership change) by design; 3rd party tools require ownership permissions to write directly
solution: Create directory on Linux host mounted via NFS, set chmod -R 777, configure NFS allowed client IPs in Data Box WebUI, then add target to Cohesity/3rd party tool
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Permission error when writing to Data Box via NFS

symptom: MARS Agent cannot locate/detect NFS share path on Data Box when configuring offline backup to Azure Recovery Services Vault
rootCause: NFS client not enabled/installed on Windows host, and/or host IP not added to NFS allowed client list in Data Box local WebUI
solution: Enable NFS client feature on Windows host, mount Data Box as Local System, add host IP to NFS allowed client IP addresses in Data Box WebUI (Connect & Copy → NFS → Allowed Client IP Addresses)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Problem detecting NFS share on Data Box being used with Microsoft Recovery Services Vault

symptom: SMB credentials on Azure Data Box share expire after 45 days; unable to access Data Box share via SMB after initial configuration period
rootCause: Default SMB credential expiration period on Data Box is 45 days; without manual override, credentials expire and block share access
solution: Connect to Data Box via MiniShell (PSSession to device IP with storsimpleadmin), enable support access, enter SupportSession, reset password with 'net user /add User01 Pass1', grant SMB share access with Grant-SmbShareAccess, set password to never expire with 'WMIC USERACCOUNT WHERE Name=User01 SET PasswordExpires=FALSE'
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/SMB Password Reset on Azure Data Box Share

symptom: Unable to access Azure Data Box / Azure Stack Edge / Data Box Gateway SMB shares due to authentication failure; SMB Session Authentication Failure with status 0xC000006D and mismatched LmCompatibilityLevel in Smbserver.Security event logs
rootCause: Mismatched LmCompatibilityLevel between client and Data Box device; client configured with lower LM compatibility level (e.g., level 0 sending LM & NTLM) while Data Box expects NTLMv2 only (level 5), causing authentication protocol mismatch
solution: Change LAN Manager authentication level on client: secpol.msc → Local Policies → Security Options → Network Security: LAN Manager authentication level → 'Send NTLMv2 response only/refuse LM & NTLM'. If GPO blocks secpol, set registry HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\LSA\LmCompatibilityLevel to 5 and restart. First eliminate domain/group policy as cause by testing with local credentials and moving machine to clean OU
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Unable to access data box share Permissions - NTLM configuration issues

symptom: Cannot access or navigate folders/files copied to Data Box with robocopy ACL preservation flags (/B /MIR /IT /COPY:DATSO /DCOPY:DAT); permission errors when browsing SMB shares via File Explorer after data copy
rootCause: Data Box is not domain-joined and cannot recognize customer's domain user IDs or security principals embedded in ACLs; File Explorer does not exercise backup semantics while the Data Box internal SMB session user has backup privileges only usable by backup-aware tools
solution: Use backup-semantics-aware tools (robocopy, PowerShell) instead of File Explorer to verify files on Data Box. ACLs are preserved for upload to Azure Files. To verify ACLs, use internal ACL Compare Tool (DVTool.exe) or suppress ACL enforcement via support session (requires customer consent and TA/engineering guidance). If data already uploaded and permissions wrong at destination, customer must place new Data Box order or work with Azure Files team
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Unable to access folders and files on Data Box after preserving ACLs

symptom: Windows cannot access Data Box share - The network path was not found error when connecting to SMB/NFS shares over network
rootCause: Network connectivity issues preventing client from reaching Data Box device shares; device may be unreachable due to network configuration, cabling, or device failure
solution: 1) Ping the share name from CMD; if fail, try pinging by IP. 2) Try access from different client on same network. 3) Restart Data Box and retry. 4) Check Event Viewer for SMB/network errors. 5) Collect network trace. 6) If unresolved, collect support package and create ICM with DataBox Data path team
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Unable to connect to the Databox SMB or NFS shares over the network

symptom: Windows cannot access Data Box share - The system cannot find the specified file error; device may not respond to ping
rootCause: Hardware-related issues with Data Box device or faulty network cables preventing connectivity
solution: 1) Ping device by IP. 2) Try different assigned IPs. 3) Try from different machine on same network. 4) Check cables - try different cables. 5) Restart device and reconnect. 6) If unresolved, collect support package and create ICM with DataBox Data path team
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Unable to connect to the Databox SMB or NFS shares over the network

symptom: Windows cannot access Data Box share - You do not have permission to access error when connecting to SMB/NFS shares
rootCause: User has not been granted permission to access the Data Box share; share-level access not configured for the connecting user
solution: 1) Verify if user has share access permission. 2) Try with different host machine. 3) Try with different user who has access. 4) Grant the user access to the shares. 5) If still failing after permission grant, collect support package and create ICM
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Unable to connect to the Databox SMB or NFS shares over the network

symptom: Windows cannot access Data Box share - generic cannot access error with potential 3rd party filter driver interference
rootCause: 3rd party filter drivers installed on host machine intercepting SMB traffic to Data Box device; host machine-specific issue
solution: 1) Try access from different host with same user. 2) If works on other host, issue is host-specific. 3) Ping device IP to rule out network issue. 4) Run fltmc from admin CMD/PowerShell to check for 3rd party filter drivers. 5) Remove/disable problematic filter drivers and retry
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Unable to connect to the Databox SMB or NFS shares over the network

symptom: Failed to create Blob container on Data Box using Azure Storage Explorer - getaddrinfo ENOTFOUND or HTTP header format error
rootCause: Blob service endpoint not added to hosts file causing DNS resolution failure; or Storage Explorer not configured to target Azure Stack APIs
solution: 1) Verify blob service endpoint is in hosts file (Windows: C:\Windows\System32\drivers\etc\hosts, Linux: /etc/hosts). 2) Add endpoint from Data Box Web GUI Access Storage account section. 3) Connect via Use a Storage account or Use a connection string. 4) For HTTP header error: select Edit > Target Azure Stack in Storage Explorer and restart
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Unable to create a container on the Data Box using Azure Storage Explorer

symptom: Unable to delete or rename files/folders on Data Box device; errors about invalid characters, Administrator permission, or root directory deletion
rootCause: Folders with invalid characters or improperly copied folders in root directory; user lacks permissions; UAC blocking deletion
solution: 1) Ensure device unlocked, login with domain admin. 2) Disable UAC and reboot. 3) Create empty temp: mkdir c:\temp\empty. 4) Robocopy purge: robocopy.exe c:\temp\empty <target_parent> <dir> /purge /B. 5) Verify in Explorer. 6) If persists, collect Process Monitor logs and ICM DataPath team
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Unable to delete files or folders from Data Box

symptom: Unable to mount NFS share on Azure Data Box from Linux host; cannot copy data via NFS
rootCause: NFS client IP not whitelisted in Data Box local web UI; NFS client not installed on Linux host; or files copied directly to root folder instead of subfolder
solution: 1) In Data Box local web UI > Connect and copy > NFS settings > add client IP. 2) Install supported NFS client for Linux distro. 3) Mount: sudo mount <IP>:/<share> <mountpoint>. For Mac: add -o sec=sys,resvport. 4) Always create subfolder under share before copying
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Unable to mount NFS share of Azure Data Box

symptom: Windows cannot access Data Box SMB/NFS share over network; error messages include "The network path was not found", "The system cannot find the specified file", or generic "Windows cannot access the share"; unable to reach Data Box shares from host machine
rootCause: Network connectivity issue between host and Data Box device: DNS/IP resolution failure, faulty Ethernet cables, 3rd party filter drivers (antivirus, backup agents) intercepting SMB traffic, or Data Box device not responding on assigned IP
solution: 1) Ping Data Box IP from host to verify network reachability. 2) If ping fails, try different IP assigned to Data Box, try from different host on same network. 3) Check Event Viewer (Application/System/SMBClient) for errors. 4) Check cables - try different cables. 5) Restart Data Box device and retry. 6) Run "fltmc" from admin CMD to check for 3rd party filter drivers interfering with copy. 7) If still failing, collect network trace + support package and create ICM to DataBox Data path team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Unable to connect to the Databox SMB or NFS shares over the network

symptom: Windows cannot access Data Box share - "You do not have permission to access" error when connecting to SMB shares on Data Box device
rootCause: User account has not been granted permission to access the Data Box SMB share; share-level access control blocks the connection
solution: 1) Verify user has been granted share access on Data Box. 2) Try connecting with a different user who has access. 3) If other user works, add the original user to the share permissions. 4) If still not working after granting permissions, collect support package and create ICM to engineering.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Unable to connect to the Databox SMB or NFS shares over the network

symptom: Unable to create Blob container on Data Box using Azure Storage Explorer; errors: "Failed to create Blob container getaddrinfo ENOTFOUND" or "The value for one of the HTTP headers is not in the correct format"
rootCause: DNS resolution failure: Data Box Blob service endpoint not added to local hosts file, or Azure Storage Explorer not configured for Azure Stack API (causing HTTP header format errors)
solution: 1) Add Data Box Blob service endpoint to hosts file (Windows: C:\Windows\System32\drivers\etc\hosts; Linux: /etc/hosts). 2) Get endpoint from Data Box local Web UI > Access Storage account. 3) In Storage Explorer connect using connection string from Web UI. 4) For HTTP header format error: Edit > Target Azure Stack and restart Storage Explorer. 5) Ref: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/databox/data-box-deploy-copy-data-via-rest
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Unable to create a container on the Data Box using Azure Storage Explorer

symptom: Unable to delete or rename files/folders on Data Box device; errors include Administrator permission required, invalid characters in folder names, or deletion fails in root directory of share
rootCause: Folders with invalid characters or improperly copied files placed in root directory; user lacks required permissions; UAC may block deletion on the device
solution: 1) Verify device unlocked, user logged in with domain admin. 2) Disable UAC and test. 3) Reboot pod and retry. 4) Robocopy purge: mkdir c:\temp\empty, then robocopy.exe c:\temp\empty <target_parent> <dir_to_delete> /purge /B. 5) Verify in Explorer. 6) If persists, collect Process Monitor logs and ICM to DataBox DataPath team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Unable to delete files or folders from Data Box

symptom: Unable to mount NFS share on Azure Data Box from Linux host; NFS mount command fails; cannot copy data to Data Box over NFS
rootCause: NFS client IP not whitelisted in Data Box NFS settings; NFS client package not installed on Linux host; incorrect mount command syntax (Mac clients missing sec=sys,resvport options)
solution: 1) Data Box local Web UI > Connect and copy > NFS settings > NFS client access: add Linux host IP. 2) Install NFS client on Linux host. 3) Mount: sudo mount <IP>:/<share> <mount_point>. 4) For Mac: sudo mount -t nfs -o sec=sys,resvport <IP>:/<share> <mount>. 5) Always create subfolder under share before copying (subfolder becomes container in Azure). Ref: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/databox/data-box-deploy-copy-data-via-nfs
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Unable to mount NFS share of Azure Data Box

symptom: Data Copy Service fails with error 'Could not complete the copy job' when source storage is Dell Isilon OS and target is Azure File Storage on Data Box
rootCause: Dell Isilon OS does not support System Access Control Lists (SACLs). Data Copy Service attempts to copy both DACLs and SACLs to Azure Files; since Isilon does not support SACLs and there is no option to skip them, the operation fails
solution: 1) Change target storage from Azure File Storage to Block Blob in Data Copy Service. 2) Alternatively, mount SMB share on Data Box and use robocopy: net use \\[DataBoxIP]\[share] /u:[DataBoxIP]\[user], then robocopy [Source] [Target] * /e /r:3 /w:60 /is /nfl /ndl /np /MT:32 /fft /B /Log+:[LogFile]. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/databox/data-box-deploy-copy-data
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Data Copy Service/Data Copy Service Failure with Dell Isilon OS as Storage Source

symptom: Data Box device received at Azure datacenter but data copy to storage account has not started; order status shows Received but no progress on data transfer
rootCause: Multiple possible causes: shipping delay (device not yet at DC), delay at Azure DC in moving job to next state, device hardware issue, multiple retries due to DC issue, customer changed/deleted storage account, unit not connected with network adapters at DC, unit damaged in transit
solution: 1) Check ASC: verify AtAzureDC stage status shows succeeded. 2) Check Shipping Status in ASC for shipping details. 3) Check Data Transfer Status in ASC. 4) If issue persists, create support case under: Windows Azure > Azure Databox > Technical > Issues in Datacenter > DC operations > select topic based on exact issue
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Data Copy to Azure/Data Box copy not yet started

symptom: Data Box device lost during return shipping; device shipped by customer has not been received at Azure datacenter
rootCause: Device was lost by shipping carrier (FedEx/UPS/DHL) during return transit to Azure datacenter
solution: 1) Check tracking in ASC: Resource Explorer > Shipping Status > reverse shipping type for return tracking URL. 2) Check courier details (FedEx/UPS/DHL) using tracking URL. 3) If courier shows no progress/movement, create ICM with DBX Operations Team for assistance. Ref: How to create an IcM for Data Box wiki page
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Data Copy to Azure/Data Box device lost

symptom: Data copied to Data Box and uploaded to Azure Storage Account without errors, but data size shown on Azure Portal (Container Properties > Calculate Size) differs from what was copied to the device
rootCause: The Calculate Size feature on Azure Portal Storage Account Container properties is unreliable and may not reflect exact data size values
solution: 1) Verify no copy errors from Data Box upload. 2) Use more reliable methods to check actual container size: a) Azure Support Center: Storage Account > Capacity > Account Capacity Summary, b) Azure Storage Explorer: Containers > select container > Folder Statistics (recommended for customers), c) PowerShell: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/scripts/storage-blobs-container-calculate-size-powershell
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Data Copy to Azure/Data copied without errors from Databox but size seems different in Storage Account

rootCause: The file optimization feature enabled by default in Data Copy Service WebUI packs files smaller than 1 MB during ingestion to speed up data copy for small files, creating ADB_PACK_* packed files
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Data Copy Service/Files copied in an unknown format (ADB_PACK_*) when using the built-in Data Copy Service
solution: Do not delete or modify ADB_PACK_* files; they are temporary and will be automatically unpacked and restored to original format when data is uploaded from Data Box to Azure Storage
symptom: Files copied via Data Box Data Copy Service appear as unknown files with ADB_PACK_* format instead of original file names

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Data Copy to Azure/Data copy from Data Box to correct storage account but wrong storage type
solution: No server-side fix. Use AzCopy to move data between blob and file storage: 1) Identify where data was ingested (Storage Explorer or BOM files). 2) Generate SAS token. 3) Run: azcopy copy blob-url file-url --recursive. 4) Verify then delete source. No refund for user error.
symptom: Customer copies data to wrong folder on Data Box (e.g., block blob folder instead of Azure Files or vice versa), data ingested into wrong storage type
rootCause: User error: data copied to incorrect storage type folder on Data Box device (e.g., block blob share instead of file share)

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Data Copy to Azure/File uploaded in lease expired state were unavailable on file share
solution: 1) Create ICM with DBX Operations for Hold for Investigation. 2) Verify no other app has lease. 3) Check if using Data Box Gateway or File Share syncing. 4) Complete file share sync, unmount share, disconnect Gateway, remap drives, reconnect. 5) Verify files accessible.
symptom: Data Box upload completes but some files fail with Error Code 412: There is currently a lease on the blob and no lease ID was specified. Blobs in Lease Expired state
rootCause: Another application (Data Box Gateway, File Share Syncing) acquired lease on target blobs, or data churn during upload. Duplicate data to same container from prior DBX disk upload.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Data Copy to Azure/Size on Data Box differs from size in Azure Storage Account
solution: Check source NAS for physical vs logical size. Explain: logical size (Azure) is smaller because Data Box allocates by physical size, Azure stores logical. File count match confirms successful copy.
symptom: Data copied to Data Box and uploaded to Azure, file count matches but total size on NAS source differs significantly from Azure Storage size
rootCause: NAS devices (EMC Epsilon/Isilon) report physical size (with data protection) vs logical size (actual content). Data Box uses physical size, Azure stores logical size.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Device Return - Logistics/Customer dropped off device as USPS instead of UPS
solution: 1) Create ICM with Operations Team. 2) Email adbops@microsoft.com with order details. 3) If device lost, share pricing: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/databox/
symptom: Customer shipped Data Box via USPS instead of UPS, tracking unavailable, device location unknown
rootCause: Customer used wrong carrier (USPS) instead of designated carrier (UPS)

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Device Return - Logistics/Device Lost by UPS
solution: 1) Create ICM with Operations Team. 2) Email adbops@microsoft.com with order/tracking details. 3) If investigation too long, customer re-creates job (no charge for incomplete job).
symptom: Data Box returned via UPS/DHL but delivery delayed beyond expected timeframe, device not received at datacenter
rootCause: Device delayed or lost by carrier (UPS/DHL) during return transit

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Device Return - Logistics/Device Pickup Issues
solution: 1) Create ICM with Operations Team. 2) Email adbops@microsoft.com to arrange collection. 3) Ops team arranges pickup without fee. 4) Follow up on collection date.
symptom: In Europe, UPS/DHL requests pickup fee from customer for Data Box return, even though return service is included
rootCause: Carrier incorrectly charging collection fee; device return service is included in Data Box pricing

symptom: Data Box export order fails or no data exported due to incorrect XML file formatting; XML tags case-sensitive mismatch, opening/closing tags not matching, or invalid blob/file prefixes
rootCause: XML file used for Data Box export order has formatting errors: tags are case-sensitive and must match exactly; incorrect formatting leads to data export failure with no data exported
solution: Follow the sample XML file table specifications from Microsoft documentation; ensure XML tags are case-sensitive match, opening and closing tags match, and blob/file prefixes are valid. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/databox/data-box-deploy-export-ordered
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Export Jobs/Assistance with creation of XML file

symptom: Data Box export order error when attempting to export managed disks; log file not loading, requested VHD file not copied to device
rootCause: Exporting managed disks via Data Box is not a supported use case
solution: Inform customer that managed disk export via Data Box is not supported; customer needs to cancel the order and use an alternative method (e.g., export disk as VHD to storage account first, then use Data Box)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Export Jobs/Data Box Export order error for managed disk

symptom: Data Box export job from Azure halted or fails; files not copied during export; etag mismatch in copy logs between beforeExportEtag and afterExportEtag
rootCause: Data churn during export operation: files (e.g., VHDs) were modified by running VMs while the export was in progress, causing etag changes that halt the copy process
solution: Create ICM with Data Box Operations to ship device as-is so customer can check exported data; for failed files, customer must create new export order and ensure no VM writes to VHDs during export operation; check copylog container in storage account for etag mismatch details
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Export Jobs/Data churn impacting export job

symptom: Data Box device unable to power up on arrival; no activity lights; system fault indicator LED is red indicating fan failure, high CPU/motherboard temperature, or DIMM ECC error
rootCause: Hardware failure: possible fan failure, CPU temperature too high, motherboard temperature too high, or DIMM ECC error; device may be dead on arrival (DOA)
solution: Verify proper power connection and try different outlet; check front panel LED indicators (bottom LED = system fault indicator); if red: check fan, move device to location with greater airflow; if unresolved, create ICM with Data Box Ops team to arrange device return/replacement
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Hardware/Data Box dead on arrival (DOA)

symptom: Data Box power supply unit (PSU) is faulty; device does not power on or has intermittent power issues
rootCause: No field replaceable units in Data Box; any hardware issue including PSU failure requires device return
solution: Customer must return the device; there is no field replaceable unit. Create ICM with Data Box Ops team to arrange return and replacement device
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Hardware/Hardware FAQ

symptom: Data Box Pod delivered without power cable; no power cord included in shipment
rootCause: Power cables were not included during shipping/packaging of the Data Box device
solution: Create ICM with Data Box Ops team to either ship the missing power cables to the customer, or customer can use any standard desktop computer power cord as a temporary solution
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Hardware/No power cord shipped with Data Box

symptom: Unable to insert, remove, or manipulate SFP+ cable or transceiver module on Data Box device; SFP+ module stuck in DATA1/DATA2 port
rootCause: Physical handling issue with SFP+ modules on Mellanox ConnectX-3 Pro EN Dual-Port 10GBASE-T adapter; customer may be using incompatible cables or handling incorrectly
solution: Customer should engage their network team or cable/transceiver vendor for physical handling assistance; recommend using supported Mellanox cables/switches tested in-house; if SFP is stuck and cannot be removed, create ICM with Data Box Ops team to arrange device return
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Hardware/Unable to insert or remove SFP cable on Data Box

symptom: Data Box Local UI shows OOPs Something went wrong with error code #60XZY (X=3 hardware unhealthy); virtual disk failed or all network adapters failed
rootCause: Hardware component failure on Data Box device - virtual disk in failed state or all network adapters failed
solution: Collect support package from limited OOBE UI; contact management and platform team; device may need replacement
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Management and How-To/Local UI Error Codes

symptom: Data Box Local UI shows OOPs Something went wrong with error code #60XYZ (Y=2 software unhealthy); DV filter service not running or exception unlocking disks
rootCause: Software health failure - DV filter service not running, exception trying to unlock disks, or pod startup tasks failed
solution: Collect support package; contact management and platform team; try rebooting the appliance
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Management and How-To/Local UI Error Codes

symptom: Data Box Local UI shows OOPs Something went wrong with error code #55XYZ; management cluster resource not running or responding
rootCause: Management cluster resource on Data Box device is not running or not responding
solution: Reboot the appliance; if persists collect support package and contact management and platform team
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Management and How-To/Local UI Error Codes

symptom: Data Box Local UI shows OOPs Something went wrong with error code #70111; unexpected error for a UI action
rootCause: Data Box returned unexpected error for a UI action; transient or unknown software issue
solution: Reboot the appliance and retry the operation
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Management and How-To/Local UI Error Codes

symptom: Data Box Download file list (BOM) button not shown in Local UI or greyed out after Prepare to Ship
rootCause: UI rendering issue or Prepare to Ship did not complete properly
solution: Refresh page; log off and log in again; retry Prepare to Ship by unlocking device first
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Management and How-To/Unable to download BOM Files

symptom: Data Box Download file list (BOM) button visible but clicking results in error; BOM file not present
rootCause: BOM file not generated during Prepare to Ship process due to incomplete or failed preparation
solution: Retry Prepare to Ship: unlock device first then perform Prepare to Ship; if persists collect support package
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Management and How-To/Unable to download BOM Files

symptom: Need to change shipping address for Data Box order after it has been placed; edit shipping address option unavailable after device dispatched
rootCause: Shipping address edit is only available in Azure portal before the device is dispatched; once dispatched the self-service option is no longer available
solution: Go to Order details > Edit shipping address to edit and validate the new address before dispatch; if device already dispatched, contact Data Box Operations team for further assistance
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Ordering - Logistics/Change Shipping Address

symptom: Data Box order showing incorrect region (e.g., West US) instead of customer-selected region (e.g., East US); order created in unexpected deployment region
rootCause: Data Box devices are allocated at nearest data center with in-stock availability; in the US, order deployment region always shows WestUS because Data Box product team is based in Western US; this only affects the Data Box resource/order metadata, not the target storage account
solution: This is expected behavior; verify target storage account regions in ASC summary tab; order deployment region for US orders is always WestUS regardless of storage account location; data will be uploaded to the storage account region specified in the order
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Ordering - Logistics/Data Box order showing incorrect region

symptom: Customer requests Data Box to be shipped to a different address than original order; need to redirect delivery destination after order placement
rootCause: Address change after order placement requires Data Box Operations team approval and coordination; cannot be fully self-serviced through portal
solution: Create ICM to Data Box Ops team requesting address change with order details; Ops team will seek stakeholder approvals and process the request; for return shipment from different address, request new shipping label from Ops team since original label has different return address
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Ordering - Logistics/Shipping Data Box to different address

symptom: Data Box device not delivered on expected date by courier service (UPS/DHL); delivery delayed beyond expected timeframe; customer reports missed or late delivery
rootCause: Regional carrier (UPS/DHL) delivery delay or missed delivery; tracking shows dispatched but device not received by customer
solution: Raise ICM (Sev3) to Data Box Ops team to open investigation case with carrier; also email Operations team using template: Subject 'Azure Data collection | <order-name>' with customer info, ICM ID, order details, serial number, and reverse tracking number from ASC
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Ordering - Logistics/The Data Box was not delivered on the expected date by courier service

symptom: Customer unable to download Data Box shipping label from portal or needs new shipping label for return; customer and device in different locations
rootCause: Shipping label unavailable in portal or customer needs label for different return location
solution: Verify Prepare to Ship completed without errors; for EU orders check e-ink display for label; if unavailable, create ICM for Data Box Ops Team and email adbops@microsoft.com with order details and ICM#
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Prepare to Ship/Customer needs new shipping label

symptom: Data Box Prepare to Ship stuck; hcsdpservice.Primary logs show SE_METADATA_NOT_FOUND error; process does not complete
rootCause: Files copied to Data Box were in use by another application or process during copy, causing metadata corruption (SE_METADATA_NOT_FOUND)
solution: Collect support package; search hcsdpservice.Primary logs for SE_METADATA_NOT_FOUND; if error points to a file, delete and recopy it; if error points to a folder, delete entire folder and recopy
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Prepare to Ship/Prepare to Ship Stuck with SE_METADATA_NOT_FOUND error

symptom: Unable to run Prepare to Ship on Data Box due to 502 error on Local Web UI; cannot access local UI to manage device
rootCause: Known issue where Data Box Local Web UI returns 502 error, preventing access to the management interface and Prepare to Ship process
solution: If data copy is complete, shutdown device using power button and return to Azure DC; create ICM with DBX Pod Management team to confirm; request return shipping labels from Ops team; DO NOT power on again after shutdown as shares will be locked
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Prepare to Ship/Unable to run prepare to ship due to 502 error on Local Web UI of Data Box

symptom: Data Box Prepare to Ship fails with ERROR_CONTAINER_OR_SHARE_CAPACITY_EXCEEDED when uploading more than 5TB to Azure File Share
rootCause: Azure storage account file share capacity limit of 5TB exceeded; large file shares not enabled on the storage account
solution: Enable large file shares on storage account (Configuration > Large file shares > Enabled); enable Disregard LFS errors in local Web UI (Connect and Copy > Settings); re-run Prepare to Ship; if shipping label not shown, download from Azure portal or create ICM with Ops
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Prepare to Ship/Uploading shares that are larger than 5 TB to Data Box Share

symptom: Customer receives incorrect files in Azure Import-Export export job; unwanted snapshots fill the drives instead of intended data
rootCause: Customer selected Export all option instead of specific files/containers; all snapshots in storage account (including Azure Site Recovery snapshots) exported until drives full
solution: Use Selected containers and blobs or Export from blob list file (XML) option instead of Export all; if using Azure Site Recovery, temporarily stop or modify snapshot retention settings before export
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Import-Export Service/Incorrect Files Sent to Customer for Export Job

symptom: Unable to create Azure Import-Export import job in portal; error related to journal file (.jrn) from waimport.exe tool
rootCause: Journal file is either incomplete (missing XML output at end) or exceeds the 2MB limit (waimport.exe v1 only)
solution: Check journal file for complete XML at end; if incomplete: v1 - regenerate with /skipwrite parameter; v2 - recopy data to drives; if exceeds 2MB limit: use the _DriveInfo_<SerialID>.xml file instead to create the import job
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Import-Export Service/Unable to Create Import Job due to Journal File

symptom: Need access to Config Hub for Azure Storage Devices root cause classification
rootCause: User not a member of Confighub-Product-Author role
solution: Go to CoreIdentity (https://coreidentity.microsoft.com) and request Confighub-Product-Author membership
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Root Cause Tree/Request Access to Config Hub

symptom: Unable to generate activation key for Azure Stack Edge (ASE); 403 error: Resource was disallowed by policy
rootCause: Azure Policy blocking creation of public endpoints, or user account lacks owner/contributor rights on the ASE resource
solution: Verify account has owner or contributor rights; check for Azure Policy blocking endpoint creation; remove or adjust the restrictive policy to allow activation key generation
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Activation Key/403 Error when Generating Activation Key
Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Hardware/Data Disk and PSU Replacement
solution: Open an ICM with Data Box Edge Platform team for any data disk and PSU replacements; these are FRUs handled by the platform team
rootCause: Data disks and PSUs are Field Replaceable Units (FRUs) that may fail and require replacement
symptom: Need to replace data disk or power supply unit (PSU) on Azure Stack Edge device; hardware component failure
Customer wants to monitor Azure Stack Edge hardware temperature; temperature not available in Azure portal metrics
rootCause: Temperature monitoring is not included in Azure Stack Edge available metrics; only accessible via BMC (iDRAC) connection
solution: Configure BMC interface via PowerShell with IP/Gateway/Subnet/Password; connect BMC port to host machine; access iDRAC portal at BMC IP with edgeuser or edgesupport credentials to view temperature dashboard
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Hardware/Monitor Hardware Temperature on Azure Stack Edge

symptom: Customer running Azure Private 5G Core (AP5GC) or AKS on Azure Stack Edge; case may need rerouting to Azure For Operators (A4O) team
rootCause: AP5GC and AKS on ASE issues are owned by Azure For Operators team, not Azure Storage Devices team; AP5GC uses custom AKS deployment instead of native Kubernetes
solution: Ask customer if running 5G workloads on ASE; if yes, reroute case to Azure For Operators support team; SAP for AP5GC and AKS on ASE: Azure -> Azure Private 5G Core
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Identifying AP5GC Cases and Customers for Rerouting and Collaboration

rootCause: K8s VMs default to 4GB memory; custom VMs depend on image used; workload requires more memory than allocated
solution: Enter support session; Get-VM to find VM name; Stop-VM; Set-VMMemory -VMName <name> -StartupBytes <amount>; Start-VM. For K8s VMs: get approval from ASE GPU Tiger Team first; may need to restart K8s cluster group. Memory range: 15%-60% of device spec (max 65% with env variable override)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Kubernetes/Increase K8 VM memory
symptom: Azure Stack Edge Kubernetes or custom VM showing alerts/errors for low or insufficient memory; VM performance degraded

rootCause: Bug in ASE builds 2301/2303: K8s v1.24 no longer auto-generates service account tokens; refreshing K8s dashboard certs removes old dashboard+token and new dashboard is created without token (Bug 17941654)
solution: Enable support session; RDP to ASE; edit kubernetesdashboard.yaml at G:\HcsExternal\Kubernetes\KubeVM\LinuxScripts\kubernetes\manifests\ to add Secret manifest for kubernetes-dashboard service account token; run Set-HcsKubernetesClusterDSC; wait 5-10 min for reconciliation; verify with Get-HcsKubernetesDashboardToken; download new config from WebUI
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Kubernetes/Unable to access kubernetes dashboard
symptom: Unable to access Kubernetes dashboard on Azure Stack Edge; Get-HcsKubernetesDashboardToken returns empty; neither cert nor token auth works for dashboard login

solution: Collect support package from ASE device; BIOS version can be found at \cmdlets\platform\systeminfo.txt in the support package
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Manage Device/Finding the BIOS version of an Azure Stack Edge device
rootCause: BIOS version information is not directly accessible via portal or CLI; it is only available in the support package
symptom: Need to find BIOS version of Azure Stack Edge device

solution: Connect to BMC at 192.168.100.100 (configure host adapter with static IP 192.168.100.5/24); log in as EdgeUser; open Virtual Console; Ctrl-Alt-Del to unlock; enter 50-digit Bitlocker Recovery Key. If key lost: Azure portal > ASE resource > Settings > Security > Secrets to retrieve it from Key Vault
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Manage Device/Recovering an Azure Stack Edge from the Bitlocker Recovery Screen (Local WebUI is Inaccessible)
rootCause: Device unable to boot past Bitlocker due to certain issues that occurred on the device; 50-digit Bitlocker Recovery Key must be entered to continue boot
symptom: Azure Stack Edge unable to boot to OS; device stuck at Bitlocker Recovery screen; Local WebUI inaccessible even though device appears powered on

solution: Enter support session; Stop-ClusterResource hcsmgmt; reg delete HKLM\Cluster\HCS\Datastore\WorkEngineInfo\<failed-job-guid> /f (find GUID in hcsmgmt.Primary logs); Start-ClusterResource hcsmgmt. If hcsedgeagentwriter error follows, repeat for that service. Update device to latest version to prevent recurrence
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Manage Device/Unable to Access Local Web UI
rootCause: Bug 7297787 (fixed post update 1911): hcsmgmt service fails due to ArgumentNullException when trying to restart incomplete orchestrated job; work item not found causes TimeoutException
symptom: Unable to access Azure Stack Edge Local WebUI; Lost heartbeat alerts in Azure portal; hcsmgmt service in failed state

solution: Enter Support Session; run Get-HcsNetInterface to view current MTU values per port; run Set-HcsNetInterface -InterfaceAlias <Port#> -MTU <value> to change; may need NodeID; verify with Get-HcsNetInterface again
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Networking/Changing the Network Interface MTU Value
symptom: Need to change MTU value on Azure Stack Edge network interface/port for proper network communication
rootCause: Customer internal network requires specific MTU size; ASE default MTU may not match customer network configuration; most networks auto-negotiate but some require manual setting

solution: Issue #1: Push cable firmly and hold 10sec; try bending latch tab up on RJ45 connector; secure cable in working position with cable tie. Issue #2: No field mitigation possible; customer must return device and order replacement. Both issues corrected in General Release units
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Networking/Failure to Establish Reliable Link
symptom: Azure Stack Edge Pro 2 Port 1 or Port 2 (10/1GbE RJ45) fails to establish link, links intermittently, or only links when cable is manipulated; LEDs off or intermittent
rootCause: Two known issues on Private Preview units: 1) RJ45 connector design issue causing inconsistent electrical connection; 2) LAN Transformer component has defective solder joints from degraded plating finish on SMT pins

solution: Option 1: Delete vSwitch on old port via PowerShell, restart ASE, create new vNIC with new IP, remove old vNIC. Option 2: Connect and configure IP on old port, restart ASE. Option 3: Connect to ARM, Remove-AzNetworkInterface -Force for all vNICs, disable compute on port 2, re-enable compute on port 2
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Networking/Issues with updating IP Subnet on vNET
symptom: Unable to update IP subnet on virtual network (vNET) for Azure Stack Edge VM; VM still shows old IP; new NIC creation fails; VNET shows old subnet
rootCause: Another port was previously configured for compute and disconnected after reconfiguration; ASE device moved to new location and VMs reconfigured but old port 3 compute config remained

solution: Add /dcopy:x option to Robocopy command to skip copying alternate data streams; files should then copy successfully
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Storage Gateway/Copy Error 665
symptom: Copy to Azure Stack Edge via Robocopy fails with ERROR 665 (0x00000299) - The requested operation could not be completed due to a file system limitation
rootCause: Azure Stack Edge uses ReFS internally which limits alternate data streams to 128KiB; source files with larger alternate data streams (thumbnails, metadata for images) exceed this limit

solution: Check hcsmgmt.Primary logs for BlobImmutableDueToLegalHold error; remove legal hold from the storage container following Azure docs on immutability policies; once legal hold removed, missing data will be re-uploaded automatically
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Storage Gateway/Unable to write Data to Azure Stack Edge due to Legal Hold on Storage Account
symptom: Unable to write data to Azure Stack Edge; upload failed error; hcsmgmt logs show BlobImmutableDueToLegalHold error with status 409
rootCause: Legal hold set on storage container associated with the ASE device makes blobs immutable; prevents upload/modification of data

symptom: Cannot update IP Subnet for Virtual Network on Azure Stack Edge VM; subnet shows old IP config; cannot deploy new VM or add NIC with new subnet
rootCause: Another port was previously configured for compute and disconnected, leaving stale compute configuration
solution: Option 1: Delete vSwitch on old compute port via PowerShell, restart ASE, recreate vNIC. Option 2: Reconnect Port and configure IP, restart ASE. Option 3: Via ARM, remove all vNICs (Get-AzNetworkInterface | Remove-AzNetworkInterface -Force), disable/re-enable compute on port
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Networking/Issues with updating IP Subnet on vNET

symptom: Robocopy to Azure Stack Edge fails with ERROR 665 (0x00000299): requested operation could not be completed due to file system limitation
rootCause: Azure Stack Edge uses ReFS internally with 128kiB limit on alternate data streams; source files with large thumbnail/metadata streams exceed this limit
solution: Add /dcopy:x flag to Robocopy command to skip copying alternate data streams
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Storage Gateway/Copy Error 665

symptom: Unable to upload files to Azure Stack Edge share; 2998 error in ManifestsPerShare Errors.xml; hcsedgeagentwriter logs show WINDOWS ERROR (0x5) at DvFilterWrapper::OpenVectorHandle
rootCause: The datadata folder on ASE share root has ReadOnly attribute set, blocking the writer process
solution: Create empty non-readonly folder on host; robocopy it to share root with /nodcopy option to reset directory attributes; wait ~15 mins for writer to retry
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Storage Gateway/Unable to upload files on ASE due to readonly being set on the share's datadata folder

rootCause: File system corruption from disk resize operation on ASE VM
solution: Connect to VM console; Option 1: create repair VM, attach disk, attempt fix; Option 2: rebuild VM from scratch
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Virtual Machines/VM Fails to Boot Up after Resize
symptom: VM on Azure Stack Edge fails to boot after disk resize; OS error 0xC0000098 required file missing

rootCause: Windows Server trial version expiration: wlms.exe initiates automated shutdown after 180 days
solution: Check EventLog for wlms.exe shutdown pattern; extend trial period (up to 6 times, ~3 years); or activate with full license
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Virtual Machines/Windows Server VM Stopping on its Own
symptom: Windows Server VM on Azure Stack Edge stops unexpectedly after ~180 days; VM shows stopped in portal; repeats multiple times per day
Cannot configure web proxy with NTLM authentication from Azure Stack Edge Local WebUI; authentication option not available after device reset
solution: Configure proxy via console support session using: Set-HcsWebProxy -AuthType NTLM -ConnectionURI <> -UserName <>
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Web Proxy/Cannot Configure Proxy with NTLM Authetnication from Local WebUI
rootCause: NTLM proxy configuration was removed from Local UI; Local UI does not support NTLM Authentication proxy configuration

symptom: Unable to apply web proxy on Azure Stack Edge from Local WebUI; errors: web proxy URL not valid or could not reach web proxy server; 403 Forbidden in hcsmgmt logs
solution: Test proxy with FQDN and port, then IP and port; run diagnostics and collect support package; test connectivity from Support Session (Ping, Tracert, Test-NetConnection); check hcsmgmt_primary logs for 403 errors; ensure all required URL patterns whitelisted per Azure docs especially https://www.msftconnecttest.com/connecttest.txt
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Web Proxy/Unable to Apply Web Proxy
rootCause: Required URL patterns not whitelisted on customer proxy; specifically msftconnecttest.com/connecttest.txt URL needed for ASE web proxy validation test; proxy may require authentication or block ASE endpoints

symptom: Case misrouted to Azure Storage Devices queue; common misroutes include PST migration, on-premises data gateway, Azure VM, AKS, Azure Stack Hub/HCI cases
solution: PST migration via network upload: reroute to Security > Purview > Import Service. On-premises data gateway: reroute to Logic App/Power Apps/Power Automate. Azure VMs: reroute to VM Running on Windows/Linux. AKS: reroute to Kubernetes Service. Azure Stack Hub: reroute to Azure Stack Hub. Azure Local (HCI): reroute to Azure Local. AP5GC/AKS on ASE: reroute to Azure Private 5G Core
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Storage Devices Wiki/Common Misroutes
rootCause: Keywords like gateway, edge, Azure Stack, import-export cause routing confusion with other Azure products

symptom: Need to set up and use Az.StackEdge PowerShell module for Azure Stack Edge automation
solution: Install Azure Az PowerShell module; Connect-AzAccount; verify subscription with Get-AzContext (Set-AzContext if needed); Install-Module -Name Az.StackEdge; then use Az.StackEdge cmdlets
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Manage Device/Using Az.StackEdge CMDlets

symptom: Azure Stack Edge Local WebUI stuck on Configuring the Device screen on first access after imaging
rootCause: Bug in ASE software builds predating 2012: ConfigState set to Uninitialized or Standalone instead of Clustered after imaging
solution: Access BMC Virtual Console; verify state with Get-HcsApplianceInfo and Get-HcsControllerSetupInformation; set ConfigState to Clustered via registry Set-ItemProperty; refresh WebUI
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Manage Device/WebUI Stuck on 'Configuring the Device'

symptom: Azure Stack Edge Pro 2 Port 1 or Port 2 LEDs always off, port completely fails to establish link under any circumstances
rootCause: LAN Transformer component has defective solder joints from degraded plating finish on SMT pins (Private Preview units only)
solution: No field mitigation; customer must return appliance and order replacement unit
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Networking/Failure to Establish Reliable Link

rootCause: Cluster Virtual Disk (ClusterPerformanceHistory) resource is Offline; can occur when updating from various builds
solution: Enter Support Session; Start-ClusterResource for ClusterPerformanceHistory; confirm Online; retry upgrade from Azure Portal
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Update Issues/Azure Stack Edge upgrade failing due to Cluster Virtual Disk (ClusterPerformanceHistory) resource down
